assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, an email address, and a local Node.js 22+ or Python 3.10+ runtime when following an SDK quickstart.
No CDP Portal account, project, API key, or Wallet Secret exists yet.
Sandbox or non-custodial first success is acceptable unless a custodial route is deliberately selected.

developer_goal: Create a CDP Portal account and reach the earliest official first-success outcome for a chosen CDP product and custody model.

first_success.outcome: Unresolved among API Key Wallet account creation, User Auth wallets, custodial APIs after business verification, and CDP CLI/agentic quickstarts.
first_success.observable_signal: The API Key Wallet quickstart prints Created EVM account with a 0x address, but peer product families define different terminals and eligibility rules.

route_selection.surface: CDP Portal signup plus official CDP docs quickstarts
route_selection.rule: Select the shortest complete account-to-first-success route only after product family, custody model, auth model, and runtime are fixed.

candidates.name: Non-custodial API Key Wallet SDK quickstart (create EVM account)
candidates.reason: Official intro says non-custodial APIs are available immediately after signup, and the API Key Wallet quickstart demonstrates terminal account creation, but it is one product family among peers.

candidates.name: Non-custodial User Auth wallets
candidates.reason: Official wallets docs present a separate user-auth quickstart with different credentials, client setup, and first-success semantics.

candidates.name: Custodial APIs after verified business account link
candidates.reason: Official intro requires a verified business account linked to CDP; eligibility, actors, and first operations differ from non-custodial routes.

candidates.name: CDP CLI or Agentic Wallet CLI quickstart
candidates.reason: Official CLI docs offer create-and-fund flows that reach wallet outcomes on a different interaction surface than the SDK quickstart.

required_path.action: Sign up at the CDP Portal and open the default project.

required_fields.label: CDP Portal signup fields
required_fields.notes: Initial Setup documents signup at portal.cdp.coinbase.com, but the live portal did not expose a public field inventory (fetch blocked).

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open the CDP Portal and manage API keys for a project.

required_path.action: Choose the CDP product family and custody model for the measured first-success route.

required_fields.label: Product and custody model
required_fields.notes: Official intro separates non-custodial and custodial API sets with different eligibility.

required_path.observable_result: One product family is selected as the measured route.

required_path.action: Create the credentials required by the selected authentication model.

required_fields.label: Secret API key ID and secret
Wallet Secret
required_fields.notes: Required for API Key Wallet write operations; other auth models differ.

required_path.observable_result: Credentials for the selected auth model are available to the developer.

required_path.action: Configure the selected SDK or CLI and run the first documented operation for that product.

required_fields.label: First operation (network, account type, command)
required_fields.notes: EVM versus Solana, SDK versus CLI, and create versus fund change the terminal.

required_path.observable_result: The selected official quickstart reaches its documented first-success signal.

prerequisites.description: Node.js 22+ or Python 3.10+ is required for the documented API Key Wallet SDK path.
Custodial API groups require a verified business account linked to CDP.

external_gates.description: Business verification and account linking gate custodial API access.

unavoidable_waits.description: Business verification approval may be required before custodial routes become available.

platform_outcomes.description: CDP creates a first project automatically after Portal signup.
For the API Key Wallet route, CDP returns a created account address after createAccount.

excluded.item: Reading the full CDP API overview and authentication guide
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is not a counted developer action.

excluded.item: Funding via faucet or sending a transaction after account creation
excluded.reason: The API Key Wallet quickstart demonstrates create-account before optional fund and send steps.

excluded.item: Switching Portal from Sandbox to Live
excluded.reason: Initial Setup presents Live keys as a later go-live step.

sources.title: Welcome to CDP API
evidence_supported: Immediate signup eligibility for non-custodial APIs
Business verification requirement for custodial groups

sources.title: Quickstart: API Key Wallet
evidence_supported: API key and Wallet Secret setup

sources.title: Initial Setup
evidence_supported: API key and wallet secret download
Business link and Live switch guidance

uncertainties.question: Which CDP product family and custody model should define corpus first success?
uncertainties.impact: It changes eligibility, credentials, implementation surface, waits, and the observable terminal.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A corpus policy choice among non-custodial API Key Wallet, User Auth wallets, custodial APIs, and CLI/agentic routes, then a fresh walkthrough of that one route.

uncertainties.question: What exact live screens and required fields create a new CDP Portal account?
uncertainties.impact: Signup field counts and verification steps cannot be verified without a public form inventory or authenticated walkthrough.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A dated read-only capture of portal.cdp.coinbase.com signup fields and any email or entity verification gates.
